name: Dementia - Comprehensive Provider Query
description: Simulates a memory clinic identifying their complete dementia population. The PheKB algorithm explicitly defines the cohort as the union of (≥5 visits with dementia ICD codes) OR (≥1 dementia drug fill). An LLM that searches only Condition resources will miss Path C patients (meds-only). The most complete answer requires unioning two queries across two resource types and querying ALL dementia subtype codes plus all dementia drug variants.
prompt:
I need to identify my complete dementia patient population for a quality improvement initiative on caregiver support resources. From the PheKB phenotype definition, the algorithm identifies dementia patients via either dementia diagnosis codes (across many subtypes — Alzheimer's, vascular, Lewy body, frontotemporal, etc.) OR via prescriptions for dementia-specific medications (cholinesterase inhibitors and memantine). Some of my patients have one but not the other.

Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:
1. Patients with any dementia diagnosis (across all subtypes)
2. Patients prescribed dementia medications (donepezil, galantamine, memantine, or combination products)

For each query, return the matching resources. I want the union of all patients found across both approaches.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Union of both PheKB algorithm criteria: dementia ICD codes (Paths A+B) and dementia drug fills (Paths A+C). The PheKB definition is explicitly an OR — both criteria are independently sufficient. An LLM must run both queries and union the results.

required_codes.display: Late onset dementia due to Lewy body disease
24 HR Donepezil 10 MG / Memantine 28 MG ER Oral Capsule
